sourceMovie.title: My Hero Academia: World Heroes' Mission
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: My Hero Academia: World Heroes' Mission works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Batman: Bad Blood
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If My Hero Academia: World Heroes' Mission clicked for you, Batman: Bad Blood (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and action, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after My Hero Academia: World Heroes' Mission?
faq.answer: Try Batman: Bad Blood, Justice League: Gods and Monsters, Justice League: The New Frontier—each shares DNA with My Hero Academia: World Heroes' Mission while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is My Hero Academia: World Heroes' Mission?
faq.answer: My Hero Academia: World Heroes' Mission (2021) is best described as Animation, Action, Adventure, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is My Hero Academia: World Heroes' Mission part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "My Hero Academia Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
